prompt_text: AI tooling tools with native OpenAI integration
response:
Here are notable **AI tooling platforms that have native (built‑in) OpenAI integration**, so you can use OpenAI models without wiring the API from scratch.

### Low‑code / no‑code app & workflow builders

- **ToolJet** – Low‑code platform to build internal tools and UIs; has a native **OpenAI data source** so you can plug GPT models into apps and workflows from the UI (chat interfaces, automations, etc.).[4]  
- **Many SaaS platforms via integrations catalogs** – Platforms listed in integration hubs (e.g., SourceForge’s OpenAI integrations page, which lists >1,000 products that connect natively to OpenAI) provide built‑in connectors so you only paste an API key or OAuth token rather than code the integration yourself.[6]

### Developer‑oriented AI SDKs / agents

- **Vercel AI SDK (AI SDK OpenAI provider)** – TypeScript/JavaScript SDK for building AI apps; has a dedicated **OpenAI provider** supporting:
  - Responses, chat, completions, and embeddings APIs  
  - Built‑in support for **OpenAI tools** (code interpreter, file search, MCP, tool search) via `openai.tools.*` helpers[1]  
  - Simple factory methods: `openai(modelId)` or `openai.responses(modelId)`[1]  
- **OpenAI’s own Tools & Agents APIs** – Not a third‑party tool but a native “tooling system” for:
  - **Function calling** (custom tools/functions)  
  - **Web search, file search, tool_search, MCP servers, skills, shell, computer use, images**, etc.[3]  
  You enable these via the `tools` and `tool_choice` parameters when calling the API.[3]  

### Agent / tool frameworks on top of OpenAI

- Tutorials and templates around **OpenAI’s Responses API with tools** show how to create agents that call custom tools (e.g., to‑do retrievers, weather APIs) using function schemas and a loop over the Responses API.[2] These aren’t platforms but are ready‑made patterns you can adopt if you are building your own tool‑calling agents.

---

If you specify your use case (e.g., “internal dashboards,” “RPA/workflows,” “code assistants,” or “customer‑facing chatbots”), I can narrow this down to a short, opinionated list of best‑fit tools with OpenAI built in.
